mnemonics: SELECT Signal Chooses Input Lane: Think of a multiplexer (MUX) like a multi-lane highway. The SELECT signals are the road signs that direct which input lane (I0, I1, etc.) gets to merge onto the single output road (Y).
keyPoints: A multiplexer (MUX) is a combinational circuit that selects one of many input lines and directs it to a single output line.

The selection of which input to route to the output is controlled by a set of SELECT lines (also called control lines). The number of select lines (n) determines the number of inputs (2^n) it can choose from.
A multiplexer can be used to implement Boolean functions and complex logic, acting as a fundamental building block for data selection and routing in a CPU.
commonMistakes: Confusing the number of select lines with the number of inputs. For example, a 4-to-1 MUX has 4 inputs but only 2 select lines (because 2^2 = 4).
Forgetting that a MUX is a combinational circuit with no memory; its output changes based on the current inputs and select signals, with no clock signal required for its basic function.
